title: Centralised apostrophe normalization utility
description: Create a single, shared normalization function that handles all known apostrophe-like Unicode characters and use it consistently across every entry point in the codebase. Currently there are three different normalization strategies (replace-then-remove, remove-outright, truncate-at-apostrophe) scattered across matchcard_service.go, club_scraper.go, fixture_scraper.go, and import-season. The player_matcher.go doesn't handle apostrophes at all, which is a latent bug. This work item consolidates all of them.
acceptance_criteria: New utility function NormalizeApostrophes(s string) string exists in a shared package (e.g. internal/normalize/)
Function handles all known variants: U+0027 ('), U+2019 (right single quote), U+2018 (left single quote), U+02BC (modifier letter apostrophe), U+2032 (prime), backtick (`), HTML entity '
Function normalizes all variants to U+0027 (standard ASCII apostrophe) — does NOT remove them
Second utility function NormalizeForComparison(s string) string: lowercases, normalizes apostrophes, then removes all apostrophes and collapses whitespace — for fuzzy matching
player_matcher.go normalizeName updated to call NormalizeApostrophes (fixes latent bug where apostrophes in player names cause match failures)
import-season truncation strategy replaced with NormalizeForComparison
matchcard_parser.go HTML entity handling delegates to shared function
All existing tests pass — normalization produces identical results to current behavior
Unit tests cover every apostrophe variant for both functions

approach: 1. Create internal/normalize/normalize.go with two exported functions
2. NormalizeApostrophes: replace all variant apostrophes with ASCII apostrophe U+0027
3. NormalizeForComparison: call NormalizeApostrophes, then ToLower, remove all apostrophes, collapse whitespace — this matches the current most-thorough strategy in matchcard_service.go
4. Write comprehensive unit tests first — table-driven tests with every known apostrophe variant
5. Replace each file's local normalization with calls to the shared functions, one file at a time
6. After each file, run the existing test suite to verify identical behavior
7. Fix player_matcher.go — add NormalizeApostrophes call in normalizeName to handle apostrophes in player surnames (e.g., O'Brien, D'Angelo)
known_apostrophe_variants: U+2019 ' RIGHT SINGLE QUOTATION MARK (common in web content)
U+2018 ' LEFT SINGLE QUOTATION MARK (common in web content)
U+02BC ʼ MODIFIER LETTER APOSTROPHE (Unicode canonical)
technical_notes.player_matcher_bug: player_matcher.go:normalizeName currently handles periods, commas, and hyphens but NOT apostrophes. A player named O'Brien imported from BHPLTA with a curly apostrophe (O'Brien) would fail to match the same player stored with a straight apostrophe (O'Brien). This is a real bug, not theoretical — Irish surnames are common in Brighton.

testing_requirements: Unit tests for NormalizeApostrophes with all 7 known variants
Unit tests for NormalizeForComparison with mixed-variant strings
Verify: NormalizeForComparison("St Ann's") == NormalizeForComparison("St Ann\u2019s") == NormalizeForComparison("St Ann\u2018s")
Verify: NormalizeApostrophes preserves the apostrophe (does not remove it)
Verify: player_matcher now matches O'Brien to O'Brien (curly vs straight)
Full E2E suite passes — no behavioral change for existing data

context.user_story: As a developer deploying for any club, I want consistent string normalization so that clubs with apostrophes in their names (St Ann's, Queen's, King's) work reliably across all data import paths
